text.title: Chinese TYPICAL FAMILY APARTMENT Tour (Price & Cost of Living 2024)
text.u: people all over China live in all sorts
of different homes we've been in this
country for around 3 months now and
we've been living here in Beijing for a
month and it's fascinating how many of
the homes differ in shapes and sizes and
also ages and today we're going to show
you around the place that we've been
living for the last month talk about
some of the costs here in Beijing and
also show some of the other homes that
are here in the
city property in Beijing is known to be
one of the most expensive in the world
but to be honest until we got here we
really didn't believe the prices were
real we're currently in one Jing at the
moment which is in the northeast of the
city and behind us you can see lots of
different types of buildings Apartments
here as in a lot of places are priced by
square meter and also by age just over
there you can see a lot of very modern
Flats these are the newest Flats on the
edge of the city that you see going up
everywhere but in this area there's also
ones built from the 80s I think early
2000s over there and then as I say the
very modern ones in front the house that
we're living in is not a high-rise like
these it's only six stories and it's
quite big inside it's also very very
expensive when we heard the price of the
flat we were shocked but we'll save that
for the end of the video actually this
is a very unique suburb where we're
staying in and we will show it properly
in a different video but for now let's
head home
this gate to me is so illogical why is
it open that
way he's not asking how uh old are you
he said how
old good job you
heard so the the little area here where
we live is actually a gated community
there's two gates to get in and you
obviously get in with a key fob what's
interesting is these homes aren't very
new but it's still gated it's security
guarded and in the middle of the little
area here there's a courtyard with like
exercise points as well as a recycling
point and yeah it's like its own little
self-contained community in the middle
of Wang Jing just here and on that note
this is the building where we're staying
so let's head upstairs and have a look
around I actually really like that we're
staying in a smaller building not in a
skyscraper and it's really interesting I
think they're building elevators here
because our neighbors living over there
have an elevator and none of these
apartments do I'm guessing yet
something that's fascinating here is all
of the doors have different decorations
on them like when you go to apartments
in most parts of the world the outside
the corridor is kind of pretty
inconspicuous you can't tell whose house
is who but each one of these all the way
up to where we're living every door has
like a different decoration on
it I think it must be something to do
with
lock I would imagine I know that red
color is really loved in
China but we can't tell you what those
signs mean
yet this all here by the way is a lot of
the uh internet so China Telecom like
internet and telephone signals that are
going to each
floor and then you can see a lot of the
electrics around here in the the
corridor and these lights by the way we
usually in Europe or especially in the
UK have these on a sensor movement
sensor so as you walk under it the light
goes on but here as you will have just
seen it's controlled by sound so you
kind of if you talk loud or if you
go then the light comes on just
something yeah that we're not used to at
all we're leaving on the sixth
floor is the to the floor
[Music]
[Music]
okay
[Music]
for she
that's Amanda's mom our host I think
she's staying with us tonight and she
said she's making us
dinner what the
what woman your eager
m w
then I washed our clothes and towels
yesterday and she tied everything up so
uh I didn't have my towel and I thought
I'll get it in early before I forget all
my ches this
evening let's start in the lounge this
is it it's pretty big we're very shocked
at the size of the flat in general here
we have sofa area massive wardrobe there
is a wardrobe next to Matt as well
dining table another wardrobe as well is
it a wardrobe or what's it
called wardrobe yeah Ward big cupboards
cupboards yeah massive
cupboards little uh laundry
area thing I love about Beijing is that
the climate is dry so everything dries
really quickly soon as you Washi clothes
dries nice and quick it's a massive TV I
wanted to show you something which is
quite unusual for us this set
up is all for making
tea you open the
cow switch it on two times two
times
look it's all about tea here in China I
love it
okay then you switch it on
right let's head into the kitchen oh
there is a massive area here as well for
your fridge and here canand to have a
little baby we'll see her in another
video and talk more about family
Dynamics here in China because we find
it quite different and fascinating to us
as well
right really good size kitchen I really
feel like I'm selling this apartment to
you it's a good one buy
it lots of space
there's a little oven as well there
space for that
hob Russian
salt trust you to find that straight
away you CH little gingerbread man okay
I found it and the tiim
M
Tio
yep and there is some other Russian
stuff in the fridge as well
anyways there is a kitchen plenty of
space one thing I wanted to say about
the kitchen is see this this is strange
to me the radiator is above the doorway
I don't know if that's like an ad hoc
addition because there wasn't enough
space to put it
here yeah or maybe they opened out the
wall I don't know but yeah strange it's
above the wall above the
door and here we actually have three
bedrooms bedroom number one is is where
our roommate
lives good
size
then here we
have
lights
bathroom a washing machine as
well and then this is quite typical in
China the toilet and the shower are in
sort of one wet room area so there's
like a squeegee that you dry the floor
with after obviously so you don't slip
PSE over tip when you need to use the L
yeah here is the room of NCO Amanda by
the way we asked Amanda if it's okay for
us to film her house and she was totally
cool about it she asked us if she should
tidy up the house before we film and we
said do whatever is comfortable for you
don't tidy up just for us and she said I
feel comfortable as it
is lights this is actually the first
time we went in
here and the thing that I was shocked
about bathroom number
two again shower second shower over the
sink area This Time by the way I noticed
that they have Japanese toilets the ones
you like I know maybe I should ask them
if I can come and use their bathroom
think there's a washing machine there as
well but I was shocked that not just the
size of the apartment is said they have
two
bathrooms really good Siz bedroom as
well it's massive wardrobe
here and
finally our bedroom
this is
it so this is our bed which is a double
bed but it's actually a bunk bed I think
eventually this is where their daughter
Hulu may be sleeping but obviously down
here but yeah it's a bunk bed so I guess
eventually maybe they'll maybe if they
start a bigger family there'll be a
space for a second one plenty of room
for everyone and then they all work work
area yeah excuse the mess well we
decided not to tidy up as well
and then a massive cupboard again lot of
space and then I think the best part
about our room is our view over the
courtyard and then just over there in
the distance you can see the skyscrapers
of Wang Jing so that's like the center
of this part of Beijing and there
there's lots of like shops and things to
do and yeah it's just a nice place to
people watch in the evening for sure and
I think we're here in the best during
the best time of the year I'm so happy
that we managed to see the Autumn the
leaves changing just the colors of
everything is
just amazing here is my calligraphy
stuff ink brush there is a actually a
small pot for the ink the box is giant
anyway I meant to ask Amanda what is
this because I keep seeing them
everywhere and actually people selling
those things no different sizes and they
look like pumpkins
they got to do but they're hollow inside
so don't
know see there is a lift I told you
elevator plenty of space
here we don't actually know the square
footage or Square meterage of this
apartment but it's a good size three
bedroom it's got two
bathrooms and I guess that brings us on
to guess the price time what do you
reckon how much is it so we read online
before coming here that Beijing is one
of the most expensive real estate cities
in the world but we were still shocked
we're currently between the fourth and
fifth ring of Beijing which is the ring
roads that go round from the center of
the city out to the edge I think there's
six rings overall and we're in between
four and five we're actually really
close to the fif ring and the price
is around £650,000 for this flat if you
wanted to purchase it which is around I
think we'll put the exact price on the
screen but maybe $850,000
I forgot the price of the apartment but
the figure that's stuck in my head is
the mortgage that they pay every month
and it's 16,000 Qui so
about maybe two grand one and a half
Grand yeah about 1600
15500 and it's over 20
years which I was shocked about because
like obviously we used to pay mortgage
but our apartment was a lot smaller and
it's a different area we didn't live in
the capital city so what you reckon
could you afford that would you pay that
price the funny thing is about Beijing
that we found out is rent is actually a
lot cheaper so in most places IE London
as an example the property prices are
extortionately high and the rent is two
but here in Beijing you can get a
property like this according to Amanda
and ni for around
600 a month let us know if that seems
expensive but I thought in comparison to
the actual property price to buy it
seems kind of reasonable overall I don't
know the statistics as to how many
people in China rent compared to buy but
one thing I remember hearing and people
telling us that a lot of youngsters live
with a family until you know they get I
don't know middle age or stay with their
parents forever or wait until their
parents pass their apartment on but it's
really hard as well for people to
purchase the property especially like in
Vision because the prices are quite High
also interesting thing we heard about if
the family has daughter they will
approve the relationship with a guy only
if he has an apartment or has something
to you know give a good life to their
daughter I don't know how people were
actually telling us that experien that
fathers were telling us this that from
their experience that they have
daughters and their boyfriends have
apartments and that's where they feel
safe and they approve but I don't know
how whe they actually works in real life
or whether it's something more of the
past yeah I thought it was interesting
it's quite different as well at this
moment in time in China there's actually
a bit of a dip in the property market
and especially in the smaller cities
properties apparently the prices are
going down but according to people we
spoke to here in Beijing at the moment
the prices here are flat but they're not
going down and that's I guess because so
many people from all over the country as
in every country want to move to the
capital so the demand is still really
high but yeah fascinating compared to
the average wages here property prices
are extortionately high in Beijing also
I'd love to know if anyone's watching
this video from different parts of China
let us know how much the equivalent
apartment would cost there as well as in
any City you're watching from be
fascinating to compare in the comments
what you can get for your money in
different parts of the world but yeah
this is our property this is our home I
keep calling it our home it's not our
home only for a month our home for a
month It Feels Like Home it does
though well on that note thanks for
coming to see our crib and we're going
to have some de out there bye
